values.value:
== FIELD TYPE FORMATS ==
-- Text -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>Text</type> <length>255</length> <required>false</required> </fields>
-- Number / Currency / Percent -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>Number</type> <precision>18</precision> <scale>2</scale> <required>false</required> </fields>
-- Checkbox -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>Checkbox</type> <defaultValue>false</defaultValue> </fields>
-- Date / DateTime / Phone / Email / Url -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>Date</type> <required>false</required> </fields>
-- TextArea -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>TextArea</type> <length>255</length> </fields>
-- LongTextArea -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>LongTextArea</type> <length>32768</length> <visibleLines>5</visibleLines> </fields> CRITICAL: <visibleLines> is MANDATORY for LongTextArea — Salesforce will reject the deploy without it. Always include <visibleLines> on every LongTextArea field, no exceptions.
-- Picklist -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>Picklist</type> <valueSet> <restricted>false</restricted> <valueSetDefinition> <sorted>false</sorted> <value> <fullName>Value1</fullName> <default>false</default> <label>Value1</label> </value> <value> <fullName>Value2</fullName> <default>false</default> <label>Value2</label> </value> </valueSetDefinition> </valueSet> </fields>
-- MultiselectPicklist -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>MultiselectPicklist</type> <visibleLines>4</visibleLines> <valueSet> <restricted>false</restricted> <valueSetDefinition> <sorted>false</sorted> <value> <fullName>Value1</fullName> <default>false</default> <label>Value1</label> </value> </valueSetDefinition> </valueSet> </fields>
-- Lookup -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>Lookup</type> <referenceTo>ParentObject__c</referenceTo> <relationshipName>ChildObject_FieldName</relationshipName> </fields>
-- MasterDetail -- <fields> <fullName>FieldName__c</fullName> <label>Field Label</label> <type>MasterDetail</type> <referenceTo>ParentObject__c</referenceTo> <relationshipName>ChildObject_FieldName</relationshipName> <reparentableMasterDetail>false</reparentableMasterDetail> <writeRequiresMasterRead>false</writeRequiresMasterRead> </fields>
-- RELATIONSHIP NAME RULES -- <relationshipName> is REQUIRED on every Lookup / MasterDetail field. You MUST include the <relationshipName> tag. A reasonable default is {ChildObjectWithout__c}_{FieldNameWithout__c}. Apex will AUTOMATICALLY rewrite the <relationshipName> value to a unique hash-suffixed name (e.g. Reservation_AI_Message_7A3F2C) before the XML is deployed. This prevents collisions with orphan child relationships left in the org from prior failed deploys. Do NOT worry about uniqueness — just emit the simple {Child}_{Field} form and Apex handles the rest.
CRITICAL RULE: When a MasterDetail field exists, the object sharingModel MUST be ControlledByParent
All field type XML formats: Text, Number, Picklist, Lookup, MasterDetail etc.
